The Other Side of 
olLeNCE 
Voices from the Partition of India 
URVASHI BUTALIA
2
The Partition of India in 1947 caused one 
of the great human convulsions of history. 
The statistics are staggering. Twelve million 
people were displaced; a million died; 
seventy-five thousand women were said to 
have been abducted and raped; families were 
divided; properties lost; homes destroyed.
claims: trains with grievous cargoes of death

blocks.text:
8
he narrates, "There is one object that all Mano Majrans—even Lala Ram Lal—venerate. This 
is a three-foot slab of sandstone that stands upright under a keekar tree beside the pond. It is 
the local deity, the Deo to which all the villagers—Hindu, Sikh, Muslim or pseudo-Christian—
repair secretly whenever they are in a special need of blessing" (8). There was not any bad 
feeling and jealousy and enmity towards each other. All of the villagers were living as family 
members.
